mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust labor market and strong underlying economic activity, leading to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong labor market is a pillar of current expansion narrative.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward Rank Information Coefficient (IC) remains below 0.02 over any rolling 36-month period.

mechanism_narrative: When the year-over-year change in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 3.0%, it signals persistent inflationary pressures that will force the Federal Reserve to maintain or increase policy rates, negatively impacting equity valuations through a higher discount rate.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where the trigger condition is met over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in industrial production (INDPRO) and a rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a broad-based economic deterioration, signaling an imminent recession and causing a risk-off sentiment that drives equity prices lower.
evidence_refs: fact:Coincident indicators are used by NBER to date recessions.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns with a hit rate greater than 55% during walk-forward validation over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment, an acceleration in housing starts (HOUST) signals strong consumer confidence and wealth effects, which translates to broader economic growth and positive equity performance. This effect is nullified in high-rate environments.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a highly interest-rate sensitive sector.
failure_condition: The correlation between HOUST momentum and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) during periods where FEDFUNDS is below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer spending, which is the primary driver of US GDP. This strength flows through to corporate revenues and earnings, supporting higher equity prices over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending accounts for ~70% of US GDP.
failure_condition: A signal constructed from real retail sales momentum (nominal RSAFS deflated by CPIAUCSL) shows a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) from its recent low is a highly reliable, real-time indicator of a recession (per the Sahm Rule). This structural break in the labor market triggers a sharp de-risking in equities.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month moving average > 0.5pp above its 12-month low
failure_condition: In the two historical instances where the trigger was met, the S&P 500 did not experience a drawdown of at least 10% in the subsequent 60 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: An inverted yield curve, proxied by the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) being significantly higher than long-term growth expectations (proxied by stable GDP), signals restrictive monetary policy that will slow future growth, leading to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Yield curve inversion is a widely cited recession predictor.
failure_condition: Periods where FEDFUNDS > 4.0% are not followed by a negative average 60-day S&P 500 return over the 1980-2023 sample.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating industrial production (INDPRO) reflects weakening business activity and investment. This slowdown in the economy's productive capacity precedes lower corporate earnings and negatively impacts equity market returns over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO is a key component of the Conference Board Leading Economic Index.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return following a negative 3-month change in INDPRO is positive more than 50% of the time.
